shot_grammar.wide_establishing: EWS/WS — sets geography, scale, world. No dialogue. First shot of scene or act.
shot_grammar.medium: MS — waist-up. Neutral coverage. Workhorse shot for blocking and staging.
shot_grammar.medium_close: MCU — chest-up. Dialogue delivery, emotional grounding. Default for lip-sync.
shot_grammar.close_up: CU — face, head, neck. High emotional stakes. Reaction shots. Decision moments.
shot_grammar.extreme_close_up: ECU — eyes, hands, objects. Maximum tension or detail. Use sparingly.
shot_grammar.OTS: Over-the-shoulder. Conversation shot. Establishes spatial relationship between characters.
shot_grammar.POV: Point-of-view. Subjective camera. Character's eyes. Creates identification / dread.
shot_grammar.two_shot: Two characters in frame. Power dynamics, relationship. Avoid equal framing for conflict.
shot_grammar.dutch_angle: Canted horizon. Psychological unease, moral ambiguity, disorientation.
shot_grammar.insert: Close-up of object or detail. Emphasizes significance. Cut in from scene.
shot_grammar.dolly_in: Camera moves toward subject. Increasing tension, focus, revelation.
shot_grammar.dolly_out: Camera pulls back. Isolation, loss, world expanding beyond character.
shot_grammar.crane: Camera lifts or sweeps. God's eye view, spectacle, emotional release.
shot_grammar.steadicam: Fluid tracking follow. Intimacy + momentum. Character-led movement.
shot_grammar.handheld: Nervous energy, naturalism, urgency. Baker / Greengrass territory.
shot_grammar.rack_focus: Foreground to background or reverse. Shifts attention, reveals, conceals.
shot_grammar.whip_pan: Fast lateral pan connecting shots. Wes Anderson, energy transitions.
shot_grammar.smash_cut: Hard cut on peak of action or sound. Punctuation. Comedic or shocking.

lighting_grammar.three_point: Key + fill + rim. Classical coverage. Safe, readable, broadcast standard.
lighting_grammar.hard_single_key: One hard source, strong shadows. Noir, drama, moral weight.
lighting_grammar.practical_only: Lighting from sources visible in frame (lamps, windows, screens). Realism.
lighting_grammar.chiaroscuro: High contrast. Deep shadow pools. Renaissance painting. Prestige drama.
lighting_grammar.golden_hour: Warm, raking, magic. Romance, nostalgia, mortality.
lighting_grammar.neon: Colored practicals from signs/screens. Crime, neo-noir, urban night.
lighting_grammar.high_key: Bright, even, minimal shadow. Comedy, fantasy, commercial.

color_grammar.teal_orange: Hollywood blockbuster split — warm skin + cool environment. Use sparingly.
color_grammar.desaturated: Bleach bypass / silver retention. Gritty realism, war, trauma.

three_act.act1: Setup (25%). Establish world, character, ordinary life. Inciting incident at end.
three_act.act2a: Confrontation first half (25%). New world, escalating obstacles, ally/antagonist.
three_act.midpoint: Major reversal or revelation. Stakes raised. Character crosses point of no return.
three_act.act2b: Confrontation second half (25%). Dark night of the soul, all-is-lost moment.

act_structure.cold_open: Pre-title sequence. In medias res or thematic statement. Hooks before credits.
act_structure.tag: Post-climax button. Emotional resolution or comedic grace note after main action.
act_structure.tv_act_breaks: Episodic: Cold open → Act 1 → Act 2 → Act 3 → Tag. Each act ends on hook.
